text: “Nibinsa gbanko funɛ biksi bau saha digi di nar vɛ o haan, o sɔbi tɔn a biksi di o vɛ unu,

ref: You know our ancestors were told, “Don't use the Lord's name to make a promise unless you are going to keep it.”
text: “Ma funɛ nuu di nibinsa funɛ biksi ma nawaalsa saha digi di ba ta chɔk ba vɔwi naa ba fun nyɛ Koosa ni chen.

ref: When you make a promise, say only “Yes” or “No.” Anything else comes from the devil. Revenge (Luke
text: Ma la nɔɔni koo aai. Ma sɛmɛɛ a kaali nan ni, ma sɛm ta maga li sintana watɛɛ.” Yesu Fun Biksi Ba Lɛusi Wɛ (Luk

ref: You know you have been taught, “An eye for an eye and a tooth for a tooth.”
text: “Ma funɛ nuu di nibinsa funɛ biksi ba saha digi, nandigi yisɛ ma se, ma gbanko yisɛ o si. Nandigi yisɛ ma nyina, ma gbanko yisi o nyin,

ref: But I tell you not to try to get even with a person who has done something to you. When someone slaps your right cheek, turn and let that person slap your other cheek.
text: amaa n ŋɔ mau saha nɔ, nandigi ya wu tagin a nyɛ ma, ma ta bur ya wu tagin ti a nyɛ un. Nandigi mo ma nindil lɛha, nyɛ o mo ma ningal lɛha gbanko.

ref: If someone sues you for your shirt, give up your coat as well.
text: Nandigi kpa ma la sɛria bar diili dɛ ma takta wewe daha, ma nyɛ un ma takta zenzen a gbaari.

ref: If a soldier forces you to carry his pack one kilometer, carry it two kilometers.
text: Nandigi muksi ma di ma chuŋi o buna a val maal bɛgim, ma val maalisa lɛ a gbaari.

ref: When people ask you for something, give it to them. When they want to borrow money, lend it to them. Love (Luke
text: Nandigi sumi ma kundɔŋ, ma kpa nyɛ un. Nandigi daŋni o pɛŋ kundɔŋ, ma kpa pɛŋ un.” Chenani Ya Zi Daŋni Ya Dɔma (Luk

ref: You have heard people say, “Love your neighbors and hate your enemies.”
text: “Ma funɛ nuu di nibinsa funɛ biksi ma nawaalsa saha digi di ba daŋni ba dɔŋtima a ha ba dɔma,

ref: Then you will be acting like your Father in heaven. He makes the sun rise on both good and bad people. And he sends rain for the ones who do right and for the ones who do wrong.
text: Ma ya chen, Koosa naa nyɛn o tɔha ni zi nyɛ ma burgi o bisau. Waa funɛ naami nha di o chaani nibin tagna awa nibin were ni, a nyɛ doon mi ba naa la tum tum tagna awa ba naa la tum tum were daha.

ref: If you love only those people who love you, will God reward you for this? Even tax collectors love their friends.
text: Ma daŋni ba naa la daŋni ma duŋduŋ, yɛrɛ ma kaa ya a na Koosa papaha? Hali lampo nilalɛ gbanko daŋni nara chene.

ref: If you greet only your friends, what's so great about this? Don't even unbelievers do that?
text: Ma zaami ma chɛnsa duŋduŋ, yɛrɛ ma tum a kaali nara? Hali ba naa funɛ vɛ ya Ju tima, ba ya chene.

ref: But you must always act like your Father in heaven.
text: Ma nyɛ ma tusa kichaani a chi chenani ma nyana Koosa naa nyɛn o tɔha ni tuha chaani chen.”

ref: When you do good deeds, don't try to show off. If you do, you won't get a reward from your Father in heaven.
text: “Ma tum tum were a nyɛ nara ni, ma ta nyɛ a li gbampulim nara maga naali. Ma tum chen, ma wa zi na papaha a li ma kunyan Koosa naa nyɛn o tɔha ti ni.

ref: When you give to the poor, don't let anyone know about it.
text: Ma tum tum were a nyɛ nara ni, ma ta nyɛ hali ma walɛ zim o wɛ.

ref: When you pray, don't be like those show-offs who love to stand up and pray in the synagogues and on the street corners. They do this just to look good. I can assure you that they already have their reward.
text: “Ma sumi Koosa, ma ta sumi a li gbampulim ni. Nara bɔnɛ daŋni ba chiŋi yau ni awa ba bar lagmi dɛɛni nɛɛ a sumi Koosa di nara na ba a pɛgi ba. Ma nu mu sɛma. Chenani nara pɛgi ba ya ba papaha,

ref: When you pray, go into a room alone and close the door. Pray to your Father in private. He knows what is done in private and will reward you.
text: amaa ma ka sumi, ma zu ma dɛɛna ma duŋduŋ a far zɔɔnɔ a sumi ma kunyan Koosa naa na ma o duŋduŋ. Ma ya chen, o zi nyɛ ma o papahau.

ref: When you pray, don't talk on and on as people do who don't know God. They think God likes to hear long prayers.
text: “Ma sumi un, ma ta sɛm sɛm damata a chi ba naa vɛ ya Ju tim chen. Ba waa lisi di Koosa zi nu ba sɛmau domi ba sɛmu a vusi.

ref: Don't be like them. Your Father knows what you need even before you ask.
text: Ma ta tiŋ ba nyinbaan. Ma sumi Koosa, o waa piin zim kinkin naa ma daŋni o watiu.

ref: You should pray like this: Our Father in heaven, help us to honor your name.
text: Nan nyinbaan nɔwa ma zi sumi Koosa. ‘Ya kunyan Koosa, ya daŋni i sɔn naa la chaani chiŋiu.

ref: Come and set up your kingdom, so that everyone on earth will obey you, as you are obeyed in heaven.
text: Ya daŋni i wa dɔ narawa, chen ni ba zi tiŋ i tutɔ daŋni a li tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga niu a chi chenani i malaikasi ta tiŋ hin a li i tɔha ti ni chenu.

ref: If you forgive others for the wrongs they do to you, your Father in heaven will forgive you.
text: “Ma gil tum tagna naa nara tum a nyɛ ma, ma kunyan Koosa naa nyɛn o tɔha ti ni, gbanko zi gil a nyɛ mau,

ref: Instead, comb your hair and wash your face.
text: Ma ka vɔti nɔɔna, ma sɛm ma se a chaasi ma nyuksa,

ref: Then others won't know you are going without eating. But your Father sees what is done in private, and he will reward you. Treasures in Heaven (Luke
text: chen ni nara wa zi koti a zim di ma vɔti nɔɔna. Ma kunyan Koosa, naa na chenani ma tum ma duŋduŋ, o zi nyɛ ma o papahau.” Chenani Ya Zi Chiksi Kidamata A Li Koosa Tɔhɔn Ni (Luk

ref: But more than anything else, put God's work first and do what he wants. Then the other things will be yours as well.
text: O lawa ma nyɛ chenani Koosa dɔ ma awa o tutɔ tiŋi te ma bambe ni. Maa lisi nan, o zi kpa kin naa maga ma daŋniu a nyɛ ma.

ref: Don't worry about tomorrow. It will take care of itself. You have enough to worry about today.
text: Adaha ma ta nyɛ chɛ kina kimuksi ma. Wuzu maga kpak a tumawa a muksi ya. Adaha ma ta kpa chɛ kina a lagmi zinan kin ni.”

ref: Don't store up treasures on earth! Moths and rust can destroy them, and thieves can break in and steal them.
text: “Ma ta chiŋsɛ kidamata a li tɛɛnɛ nɔ maga ni. Chau awa doon zi wa chɔk au ka ŋmɛɛrsa chɔk dɛ ti a zu ŋmɛ a dooni.

ref: Instead, store up your treasures in heaven, where moths and rust cannot destroy them, and thieves cannot break in and steal them.
text: Amaa ma ka kpa kidamata a chiŋsɛ a li Koosa tɔha ti ni. Chau awa doon wa zi wa chɔk a. Ŋmɛɛrsa gbanko wa zi wa kikoti a chɔk dɛ ti a ŋmɛ a.

ref: Your heart will always be where your treasure is. Light (Luke
text: Ma bambe zi nyɛn doonani ma kidamata nyɛnu.” Chenani Ya Se Nyɛ Ya Na (Luk

ref: But when your eyes are bad, everything is dark. If the light inside you is dark, you surely are in the dark. Money (Luke
text: amaa ma ya nyuluma ma wa zi zim doonani maa la. Chen vɛɛna, ma tusa vɛɛn biriu, ma wa zi na o nyinbaan a nyuksi. Chenani ma du zi tagniu a kaali nyuluma.” Koosa Nyɛ Ya Kin Naa Maga Ya Daŋniu (Luk

ref: You cannot be the slave of two masters! You will like one more than the other or be more loyal to one than the other. You cannot serve both God and money. Worry (Luke
text: “Nibɛgim wa kisuŋi nihɛɛta nara balɛ. O zi daŋni digiu a ha digi. O si zi su digiu a vɛ digi. Chen vɛɛna, ma wa zi koti a tum a nyɛ Koosa ni a ya si tima.

ref: Don't worry and ask yourselves, “Will we have anything to eat? Will we have anything to drink? Will we have any clothes to wear?”
text: “Ma ta muksi ma titi a ŋɔ, baŋnɛ ya kaa ya na diwe a di? Baŋnɛ ya kaa ya na ni a nyɔ? Baŋnɛ ya kaa ya na kilaarɛ a laarɛ?

ref: I tell you not to worry about your life. Don't worry about having something to eat, drink, or wear. Isn't life more than food or clothing?
text: “Nan nyinbaannɛ n sɛm ma. Saha maga maa daŋni diwe awa ni awa kilaarɛ. Ma ta nyɛ kin nama muksi ma. Ma miisa wa kaali ma diwe awa nii? Ma bara wa kaali kilaarɛ ta ma laarɛ chenn?

ref: Look at the birds in the sky! They don't plant or harvest. They don't even store grain in barns. Yet your Father in heaven takes care of them. Aren't you worth much more than birds?
text: Ma nyini zimebisa la za buudaha ni. A wara par ŋmaana koo a kum kiparɛ koo a kpa a a du napagsi ni. Ya kunyan Koosa naa nyɛn o tɔha ti ni la nyini a. Ma wa kaali zimebisa ta kinkann?

ref: Can worry make you live longer?
text: Ma anti la zi gbaari o bunsa a li muksi daha?

ref: Why worry about clothes? Look how the wild flowers grow. They don't work hard to make their clothes.
text: “Yɛrɛ kilaarɛ wɛ muksi ma? Maa nyini chenani kɔsa yeligisa du. A wara tum tuma koo a ar kilaarɛ a nyɛ a titi ni.

ref: God gives such beauty to everything that grows in the fields, even though it is here today and thrown into a fire tomorrow. God will surely do even more for you! Why do you have such little faith?
text: Koosa la nyɛ kɔsa wa kilaarɛ. A wa bi a vusi ka limaŋ wa pisi, din yaa wa di a. O wati ma kaali kɔsa kinkan. O wa zi bu koti a nyɛ ma kilaarɛɛ? Ma se wa su un nɛnɛ.

ref: Only people who don't know God are always worrying about such things. Your Father in heaven knows you need all of these.
text: Kin namawa ba naa vɛ ya Ju tima yaa daŋni chen, amaa ma kunyana naa nyɛn o tɔha ti ni o zimu di maa daŋni kin ta magau.

ref: God will be as hard on you as you are on others! He will treat you exactly as you treat them.
text: Chenani ma tum a nyɛ ma dɔŋtim ni, Koosa gbanko zi tum chenu a nyɛ ma. O zi tiŋ chen nyinbaanu a chi chenani maa tiŋ ma dɔŋtima chen.

ref: You can see the speck in your friend's eye, but you don't notice the log in your own eye.
text: O chi danugil nyɛn ma dɔŋtim se ni ka dagume nyɛn ma se ni. Yɛrɛ ma kaa ya a na danugil ti ma dɔŋtim ta se ni?

ref: How can you say, “My friend, let me take the speck out of your eye,” when you don't see the log in your own eye?
text: Yɛrɛ ma ya a ŋɔ ma dɔŋtim ta ni, ma nyɛ ya lisi danugil ti a ta ma se ni ka ma kpak dagume a li ma se ni?

ref: You're nothing but show-offs! First, take the log out of your own eye; then you can see how to take the speck out of your friend's eye.
text: Ma mur ma titirɛ. Ma te a yisi dagume ti a ta ma se ni, a zi koti a na a yisi danugil ti a ta ma dɔŋtim se ni.

ref: As bad as you are, you still know how to give good gifts to your children. But your heavenly Father is even more ready to give good things to people who ask.
text: Ma ya nibin tagna, amaa ma zim chenani ma zi kpa kiwere a nyɛ ma bisi ni. Ma kunyan Koosa naa nyɛn o tɔha ti ni, waa kaali ma a la kpa wu were a nyɛ ba naa la sumi un ni.

ref: Would any of you give your hungry child a stone, if the child asked for some bread?
text: Ma naa ya nyama nan, lɔsa kpak ma bisa ka ba daŋni diwe di ba di, ma zi zer buŋkul a nyɛ bauu?

ref: Ask, and you will receive. Search, and you will find. Knock, and the door will be opened for you.
text: “Ma sumi Koosa di o nyɛ ma kundɔŋ o zi nyɛ mau. Ma daŋni kundɔŋ, o wati ma zi na unu. Ma yir un o zi wa puk zɔɔnɔu ma zu o wati.

ref: Everyone who asks will receive. Everyone who searches will find. And the door will be opened for everyone who knocks.
text: Nara maga sumi Koosa di o nyɛ ba kundɔŋ, o zi nyɛ bau. Nara maga la daŋni kundɔŋ o wati, ba zi na au. Ba yir un, o zi wa puk zɔɔnɔu ba zu o wati.

ref: Would you give your child a snake if the child asked for a fish?
text: Ba daŋni nyinsa di ba se, ma zi zer dum a nyɛ bauu?

ref: Treat others as you want them to treat you. This is what the Law and the Prophets are all about. The Narrow Gate (Luke
text: “Ma tum wɛ were a nyɛ ma dɔŋtim ni a chi chenani maa daŋni ba tum kinyɛ ma. Ma kpa chenani Ju tim chiksɛ wa Koosa witinsa maga biksi nara a lagmi dɔŋ ni a mun chen.” Nyinbaan Wewe Tiŋi (Luk

ref: Anyone who hears and obeys these teachings of mine is like a wise person who built a house on solid rock.

text: Ma ta tiŋi a chi ba. Ma ka tiŋ nyinbaan wewe naa tiŋi bɔmɛɛ. Nara tama val dooni. Ba wa tel o bɔ, Koosa zi wa nyɛ ba o mi faliu.” Bugili Nɔni Awa Naa Vɛ Nɔni Wɛ (Luk

ref: Watch out for false prophets! They dress up like sheep, but inside they are wolves who have come to attack you.

ref: A good tree produces good fruit, and a bad tree produces bad fruit.

text: Bugil weri wa kinɔn nɔn tagna. Bugil tagin wa kinɔn nɔn were.

text: Bugil naa vɛ nɔn nɔn were, ba yaa teŋ unu a dau a suk din du un ni.

ref: You can tell who the false prophets are by their deeds. A Warning (Luke

text: “Koosa wa zi wa dɔ ba naa maga wa mu wati a yir min ba kunyana. Ba naa la tum chenani mu kunyan Koosa naa nyɛn o tɔha ti ni daŋni ba tum, waa la zi wa dɔ ba.

ref: But I will tell them, “I will have nothing to do with you! Get out of my sight, you evil people!” Two Builders (Luke
text: Ba sɛm nan peti, n zi ŋɔ bau, ‘N wa kut zim ma. Ma naa la tum tum tagna nan, ma la a ka min.’ ” Chenani Nara Balɛ Sa Ba Damsa (Maak

ref: When Jesus finished speaking, the crowds were surprised at his teaching.

text: Ma Ju tima, ma waa fu zi zu dooni. Amaa ma vɛ zuuli chen. Chendaha Koosa zi wa kpa mau a yo du birsi ni. Dooni ma zi wa wi kinkan.”

ref: That evening many people with demons in them were brought to Jesus. And with only a word he forced out the evil spirits and healed everyone who was sick.

ref: Suddenly a man with leprosy came and knelt in front of Jesus. He said, “Lord, you have the power to make me well, if only you wanted to.”

ref: Jesus put his hand on the man and said, “I want to! Now you are well.” At once the man's leprosy disappeared.

ref: “I will go and heal him,” Jesus replied.
text: Yesu fun ŋɔ un, “N zi wau a wa tiibi un.”

ref: When Jesus heard this, he was so surprised that he turned and said to the crowd following him, “I tell you in all of Israel I've never found anyone with this much faith!
text: O sɛm ta fun jɛri Yesu kinkan. O fun burgi a ŋɔ zɛmɛ naa funɛ tiŋ un chen, “Wutirɛ n ŋɔ ma, n funɛ pu Ju tim nidamata ba naa se su min. Baal naa vɛ ya Ju nan, si suuli kaali ba.

ref: “Lord, my servant is at home in such terrible pain that he can't even move.”
text: O fun ŋɔ Yesu ni, “Mu kunyana, mu tumtumna nyɛn mu dam ni. O nisa awa o naŋgere sou ka o wi kinkan.”

ref: Many people will come from everywhere to enjoy the feast in the kingdom of heaven with Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.

ref: Then Jesus said to the officer, “You may go home now. Your faith has made it happen.” Right then his servant was healed. Jesus Heals Many People (Mark
text: Doone Yesu fun burgi a ŋɔ lanjima nihɛɛ ti ni, “Kila dama. Chenani i si suuli du zi tum nyɛ hinu.” O tumtum ti fun vaan na alaafɛ. Yesu Fun Tiibi Nidamata (Maak

ref: Everyone in town came out to meet Jesus. When they saw him, they begged him to leave their part of the country.

ref: Jesus went to the home of Peter, where he found that Peter's mother-in-law was sick in bed with fever.

ref: He took her by the hand, and the fever left her. Then she got up and served Jesus a meal.
text: Yesu fun zu o dɛ a la gbaŋsi o nin. Yesu funɛ ya chen, o kabilima ti peti. O fun si a piili a ya diwe di o nyɛ ba.

ref: So God's promise came true, just as the prophet Isaiah had said, “He healed our diseases and made us well.” Some Who Wanted To Go with Jesus (Luke
text: O funɛ ya chene di chenani Koosa witiina Azaaya fun sɔbi a li Koosa sɛm tɔn ti ni saha digi, chen ya wuti. O fun ŋɔ, “O titi la zi tiibi ya wichɔɔrsi ta maga.” Ba Zi Ya Yesu Gantɛl Tiŋsau (Luk

ref: A teacher of the Law of Moses came up to him and said, “Teacher, I'll go anywhere with you!”
text: Ba funɛ saksi di ba la ji, Koosa chiksɛ nibiksɛ ta digi fun wa ŋɔ un, “Mu kunyana, n saksɛu di n tiŋ hin a la doonani ii la.”

ref: Another disciple said to Jesus, “Lord, let me wait till I bury my father.”
text: O gantɛl tiŋsi ta digi fun wa ŋɔ un, “Mu kunyana, n gbanko daŋni n tiŋ hinu, amaa nyɛ mu te a la sogli mu nyana.”

ref: so the disciples went over to him and woke him up. They said, “Lord, wake up! Save us before we drown!”
text: O gantɛl tiŋsi ta fun a laasi un a ŋɔ, “Ya kunyana, yaa daŋni ya sou, la ya a ta.”

ref: After Jesus left in a boat with his disciples,
text: Yesu wa o gantɛl tiŋsi ta fun zu arŋa a kaali.

ref: a terrible storm suddenly struck the lake, and waves started splashing into their boat. Jesus was sound asleep,
text: Ba funɛ la chene, pe kpok fun wa si aa viigi. O fun a mo arŋ ti kinkan, a teŋ ni a du arŋ ti tuhu ni o daŋni o lim. Amaa Yesu funɛ chɔ dore.

ref: The men in the boat were amazed and said, “Who is this? Even the wind and the waves obey him.” Two Men with Demons in Them (Mark
text: O fun ya ba alaazibu kinkan. Ba funɛ pɛɛsi dɔŋa, “Baal ŋɔ, baŋ nibine ya un? Hali o koti a sɛm sɛma peha awa ni wati a nu o nɔ.” Yesu Fun Tiibi Alizin Tagna a Li Nara Balɛ Ni (Maak

ref: After Jesus had crossed the lake, he came to shore near the town of Gadara and started down the road. Two men with demons in them came to him from the tombs. They were so fierce that no one could travel that way.
text: Ba funɛ la ji bɛɛŋ ti jibaŋ kinkin ti, ba fun wa tel Gɛrasin tima pulum. Nara balɛ fun nyɛn dooni a kpak alizin tagna ba nyuuni ni. Nandigi wa fun kpak kpok a kaali ba. Chendaha nandigi wa fun koti a gba dooni. Yesu funɛ wa li arŋ ti ni, nara balɛ ta fun wa li doonani nara yaa sogli ba nawaalsa di ba wa chem un.

ref: Not far from there a large herd of pigs was feeding.
text: Kurchusa funɛ nyɛn kuŋkule nyuhu ni a di diwe.

ref: So the demons begged Jesus, “If you force us out, please send us into those pigs!”
text: Alizin tagni ta fun a sumi Yesu a ŋɔ, “I duŋni ya a ta, nyɛ ya la zu kurchusi ta ni.”

ref: Jesus told them to go, and they went out of the men and into the pigs. All at once the pigs rushed down the steep bank into the lake and drowned.
text: Yesu fun ŋɔ ba, “Ma la dooni.” Ba fun li a la zu kurchusi ta ni. Kurchusi ta fun si a chɔ a la wilsi a chesi bɛɛŋ ni ti ni. Ni ti fun di a maga.

ref: The people taking care of the pigs ran to the town and told everything, especially what had happened to the two men.
text: Ba naa funɛ nyini kurchusi ta chen, ba fun chɔ kaali ba tɔha a la ŋɔ ba naa nyɛn dooni chenani ya ba naa alizin tagni ta fun nyɛn chen.

ref: Jesus got into a boat and crossed back over to the town where he lived.

text: Jems awa o niinbe Jɔn, Zɛbɛdi bisa. O gbanko funɛ nyɛ ba sɔn falɛwa Boanɛjis. Ba sɔn ta muna funɛ ya nibin numa.

text: O gbanko funɛ lisi Andiru, Filip, Batɔlɔmi, Matiu, Tɔmas, awa Jems, Alfius be, Tadius, Simɔn naa o pulum wɛ fun nyɛn o bambi ni,

ref: Some teachers of the Law of Moses came from Jerusalem and said, “This man is under the power of Beelzebul, the ruler of demons! He is even forcing out demons with the help of Beelzebul.”
text: Koosa chiksɛ nibiksɛ ta bɔnɛ fun li Jɛrusalɛm a ŋɔ dɔŋ ni, “Biɛlzɛbub, naa ya alizin tagna maga koora, nyɛn un ni a la nyɛ un kpok saha maga, chen ni o zi kotiu a duŋni alizin tagni ta a li nara ni.”

ref: The crowd sitting around Jesus told him, “Your mother and your brothers and sisters are outside and want to see you.”
text: Ba fun wa zu o wati a ŋɔ un di o niin ti awa o niinbisi ta nyɛn dentil ni a pɛɛsi o wɛ.

ref: Jesus told the people to gather around him. Then he spoke to them in riddles and said: How can Satan force himself out?
text: Yesu fun yir ba a jɛɛgi jɛɛgɛ a nyɛ ba a pɛɛsi ba, “Yɛrɛ sintana kaa ya duŋni o titi a ta?

ref: Jesus went back home, and once again such a large crowd gathered that there was no chance even to eat.
text: Zɛmɛ kinkan bur lagmi a wa. Hali Yesu awa o gantɛl tiŋsi ta wa fun na nyinbaan a di diwe.

ref: A nation whose people fight each other won't last very long.
text: Tɔhɔn tima vɛ kpak nɔ bɛgim, ba dɔma wa zi wa a chɔk ba tɔhɔn tii?

ref: And a family that fights won't last long either.
text: Dam bɛgim nara burgi a yo dɔŋa, chen dam ti zi pisiu a chel.

ref: How can anyone break into the house of a strong man and steal his things, unless he first ties up the strong man? Then he can take everything.
text: “Ma vɛ te kpak nikpok a vɔwi, yɛrɛ ma kaa ya a zu o dama a fuksi o kina?

ref: I promise you that any of the sinful things you say or do can be forgiven, no matter how terrible those things are.
text: “Ma nyɛ mu ŋɔ ma nan. Koosa koti a gil nara tum tagna maga awa chenani ba vɛ ya un jirima a nyɛ ba.

ref: But if you speak against the Holy Spirit, you can never be forgiven. That sin will be held against you forever.
text: Nara vɛ nyɛ Koosa siim weri ti jirima, Koosa wa zi gil chen a nyɛ ba, hali saha naa vɛ kpak bɔ.”

ref: Jesus said this because the people were saying that he had an evil spirit in him. Jesus' Mother and Brothers (Matthew
text: O funɛ sɛm a nama domi bɔnɛ ŋɔ di alizin tagin kani o nyuhu ni. Yesu Nitiŋɛ Ya O Wuti Walɛ (Mat.

ref: Jesus' mother and brothers came and stood outside. Then they sent someone with a message for him to come out to them.
text: Doone Yesu niina awa o niinbisa fun wa tel a wa chiŋi dam ti dentil ni a tum nara di ba zu a yir un.

ref: Jesus asked, “Who is my mother and who are my brothers?”
text: Zɛmɛ ba naa funɛ kani a go un, o fun pɛɛsi ba, “Anti ya mu niin wuti awa mu niinbisa?”

ref: Then he looked at the people sitting around him and said, “Here are my mother and my brothers.
text: O fun nyini nibinsa ba naa kanɛ a go un chen a ŋɔ, “Ma na mu numa awa mu niinbisi ta nɛɛnɛ.

ref: Anyone who obeys God is my brother or sister or mother.”
text: Ba naa maga la tum chenani Koosa daŋni ba tum, ba waa ya mu numi ta awa mu niinbisi ta.”

ref: The next time Jesus taught beside Lake Galilee, a big crowd gathered. It was so large that he had to sit in a boat out on the lake, while the people stood on the shore.
text: Yesu fun bu wa bɛɛŋ ti a biksi nara Koosa wɛ. Zɛmɛ kinkan fun wa o wati a wa go un. Domi ba funɛ iiu kinkan, o fun a nyini arŋ bar nyɛni a la zu, amaa zɛmɛ ta wa funɛ chiŋi jibaŋ nɛɛ.

ref: While the farmer was scattering the seed, some of it fell along the road and was eaten by birds.
text: O funɛ zaasi chene, kiduhu ti bɔnɛ fun chesi nyinbaan ni. Zimebisa fun wa pini a naa chesi nyinbaan ni chen maga a di.

ref: He used stories to teach them many things, and this is part of what he taught:
text: O fun jɛɛgi jɛɛgɛ a biksi kidamata a nyɛ ba a ŋɔ,

ref: Now listen! A farmer went out to scatter seed in a field.
text: “Baal digi funɛ nyɛn doon a fun si a kaali o ŋmaha di o la zaasi kiduha.

ref: Other seeds fell on thin, rocky ground and quickly started growing because the soil wasn't very deep.
text: Bɔnɛ ka fun chesi buŋkul tuhu ni. Hɛɛra wa fun to dooni kinkan. Chendaha a fun nyu lalaga.

ref: But when the sun came up, the plants were scorched and dried up, because they did not have deep roots.
text: Saha naa nha fun wa num a kpak a, a fun fiimi a siti. A wa fun koti a ta luta nɛnɛ.

ref: Some other seeds fell where thornbushes grew up and choked out the plants. So they did not produce any grain.
text: Kiduhu ti bɔnɛ ka fun chesi sɔta bar nyɛni ni a nyu a zini. Sɔti ta gbanko fun nyu a zini a tɔ a. Chendaha a wa fun koti a lul.

ref: Finally, Jesus said: What is God's kingdom like? What story can I use to explain it?
text: Yesu fun bu pɛɛsi ba, “Baŋ kinnɛ ya zi koti a kpa lagmi Koosa koor ti ni a nyini a kpa maksi a koor ti?

ref: When Jesus was alone with the twelve apostles and some others, they asked him about these stories.
text: O funɛ wa ka o duŋduŋ, ba naa funɛ tiŋ un awa o gantɛl tiŋsa fidalɛ ta, ba fun wa a wa pɛɛsi un o jɛɛge ta mun.

ref: But they don't have roots, and they don't last very long. As soon as life gets hard or the message gets them in trouble, they give up.
text: amaa laaboor weri ti wa fun ta lur ta ba ni. Ba funɛ tiŋ tamnɛ, amaa saha naa namsi wa kpak ba chen laaboor weri ti daha, ba vɛ un a bu vɛ tiŋi.

ref: The reason is, “These people will look and look, but never see. They will listen and listen, but never understand. If they did, they would turn to God and be forgiven.” Jesus Explains the Story about the Farmer (Matthew
text: ‘Chen ni ba zi nuu aa kidaŋni ba zim a muna amaa ba wa kizim. Ba zim a muna, ba bambe zi chɛrgiu, chen ni Koosa zi gil ba tum tagna a nyɛ ba.’ ” Yesu Fun Ŋɔ Ba Nipari Naa Zaasi Kiduha Chen Mun (Mat.

ref: The seeds that fell along the road are the people who hear the message. But Satan soon comes and snatches it away from them.
text: Kiduhu naa funɛ chesi nyinbaan ni chen chi ba naa nu, amaa sintana funɛ siu lalaga a wa yisi laaboor ti a li ba bambe ni.

ref: What the farmer is spreading is really the message about the kingdom.
text: Baal naa funɛ zaasi kiduhu ti chen, Koosa laaboor weri tirɛ o fun a zaasi chen.

ref: Jesus also said: You don't light a lamp and put it under a clay pot or under a bed. Don't you put a lamp on a lampstand?
text: Yesu fun pɛɛsi ba, “Nar suk fitina, o zi bu kpa tempile a chik un niu koo o zi kpa un a chiŋsi o bar chiŋi niu o chaani kinkann?

ref: Listen carefully to what you hear! The way you treat others will be the way you will be treated—and even worse.
text: “Maa nu chenani n kaa ŋɔ ma nan. Ma doso a nu mu sɛma a gbaari, Koosa zi suŋi mau ma zim a gbaari.

ref: Again Jesus said: God's kingdom is like what happens when a farmer scatters seed in a field.
text: Yesu fun ŋɔ di, “Koosa koor ti chi nipari naa funɛ si a la o ŋmaha di o la zaasi o kina chene.

ref: It is the ground that makes the seeds sprout and grow into plants that produce grain.
text: Tɛɛnɛ ti titi yaa chɛrgi kiduhu ti a nyu a zini. A waa zini a peti chen, a zi kpaksi loona a lulsi.

ref: Then when harvest season comes and the grain is ripe, the farmer cuts it with a sickle. A Mustard Seed (Matthew
text: A wa bi a mak kumi chen, ba kpa chisa a kaali ŋmaha ti a la kum a.” Yesu Fun Ŋɔ Ba Kidusi Maga Kiwewe Wɛ (Mat.

ref: It is like what happens when a mustard seed is planted in the ground. It is the smallest seed in all the world.
text: O chi kiduha naa ba yir ‘mastad’ ba yaa kpa aa du chene. Waa ya kidusi maga kiwewe.

ref: But once it is planted, it grows larger than any garden plant. It even puts out branches that are big enough for birds to nest in its shade. The Reason for Teaching with Stories (Matthew
text: A gantɛl ni a si a zen a kaali bugil pum maga kɔha ni. Zimebisa yaa wa du a zaŋa a hulsi ni, domi a funɛ zenu a kpak basɔn were.”

ref: He did not tell them anything without using stories. But when he was alone with his disciples, he explained everything to them. A Storm (Matthew
text: O waa ya biksi ba wu digi a gbaari, see jɛɛgɛ duŋduŋ. O wa o gantɛl tiŋsi ta funɛ nyɛn ba duŋduŋ, o fun a biksi ba a maga muna. Yesu Fun Jiŋ Peha (Mat.

ref: That evening, Jesus said to his disciples, “Let's cross to the east side.”
text: A wuzu ti nha funɛ wa chel, o fun ŋɔ o gantɛl tiŋsi ta ni, “Ma nyɛ ya la ji bɛɛŋ ti jibaŋ kinkin ti.”

ref: Jesus used many other stories when he spoke to the people, and he taught them as much as they could understand.
text: Yesu fun a jɛɛgi jɛɛgɛ kinkan a nyɛ ba a biksi ba Koosa wɛ a chi chen, hali ba zima wa peti.

ref: So they left the crowd, and his disciples started across the lake with him in the boat. Some other boats followed along.
text: O gantɛl tiŋsi ta fun gil zɛmɛ ta a zu pu Yesu arŋ tuhu ni ba kaali. Bɔnɛ gbanko fun zu arŋ bɔnɛ a tiŋ un.

ref: Suddenly a storm struck the lake. Waves started splashing into the boat, and it was about to sink.
text: Ba funɛ la chene, pe zen digi fun wa si a viigi. O fun a mo arŋ ti kinkan, a teŋ ni ti a du arŋ ti tuhu ni o daŋni o lim.

ref: Jesus was in the back of the boat with his head on a pillow, and he was asleep. His disciples woke him and said, “Teacher, don't you care that we're about to drown?”
text: Yesu fun chɔ arŋ gantɛl ni a sul nyusula a chɔ do. O gantɛl tiŋsi ta fun laasi un a pɛɛsi, “Ya kunyana, ya daŋni ya sou. I bak wa to ya nii?”

ref: Jesus asked his disciples, “Why were you afraid? Don't you have any faith?”
text: Yesu fun pɛɛsi o gantɛl tiŋsi ta, “Yɛrɛ maa ɔm chen ni? Ma se wa su minn?”

ref: Now they were more afraid than ever and said to each other, “Who is this? Even the wind and the waves obey him!”
text: O fun a ya ba alaazibu kinkan. Ba funɛ pɛɛsi dɔŋa, “Baal nɔ, baŋ nibine ya un? Hali o koti a sɛm sɛma peha awa ni wati a nu o nɔ.”

ref: She had said to herself, “If I can just touch his clothes, I will be healed.”
text: “N fu koti a la gbaŋsi o kilaari nɔ, n fu zi na alaafɛu.”

ref: Jesus said to the woman, “You are now well because of your faith. May God give you peace! You are healed, and you will no longer be in pain.”
text: Yesu fun ŋɔ un, “N be, i si suuli nyɛ i na alaafɛu. Kila dama awa tu sɔɔn ni. I wichɔɔrti ti petiu. O wa zi bu bur wi.”

ref: She had gone to many doctors, and they had not done anything except cause her a lot of pain. She had paid them all the money she had. But instead of getting better, she only got worse.
text: Hali luri tima nidamata fun a tiibi un a wa ku. O fun chɔk o malɛ maga ka o nin ti vɛ peti a fun bukoo gbaari.

ref: Then Jesus went inside and said to them, “Why are you crying and carrying on like this? The child isn't dead. She is just asleep.”
text: Yesu fun la zu dam ti a pɛɛsi ba, “Yɛrɛ ma chɛɛmi a la wi? Be ti wa sou. O chɔ dore.”

ref: Jesus and his disciples crossed Lake Galilee and came to shore near the town of Gerasa.
text: Ba funɛ la ji bɛɛŋ jibaŋ kinkin ti, ba fun wa tel Gadaria tima pulum.

ref: from the graveyard where he had been living. No one was able to tie the man up anymore, not even with a chain.
text: Nandigi wa fun koti a vɔu un. Ba yaa fun kpa zɔglɛ zenewa a vɔu un. O yaa fun a kasi a.

ref: He had often been put in chains and leg irons, but he broke the chains and smashed the leg irons. No one could control him.
text: Ba yaa fun kpa zɔglɛ a vɔti o naŋgere awa o nisa hali ba wa ku, amaa o yaa fun kasi a magau. Nandigi wa fun kpak kpok a kaali un.

ref: Night and day he was in the graveyard or on the hills, yelling and cutting himself with stones.
text: O fun chɔ kaali di o la nyɛn doonani ba yaa sogli ba nawaalsa chen awa kuŋkule nyuuna bar nyɛni ni saha maga, a la huusi a zer buŋkula aa teŋsi o titi.

ref: He then begged Jesus not to send them away.
text: O funɛ sumi Yesu kinkan di o ta duŋni a a li ba pulum ni.

ref: Over on the hillside a large herd of pigs was feeding.
text: Kurchusa funɛ nyɛn kuŋkule nyuhu ni a di diwe. A funɛ tel tusugsa lɛu.

ref: So the evil spirits begged Jesus, “Send us into those pigs! Let us go into them.”
text: Alizin tagni ta fun a sumi Yesu di o nyɛ a la zu kurchusi ta ni.

ref: Jesus let them go, and they went out of the man and into the pigs. The whole herd of about
text: Yesu fun nyɛ a nyinbaan. A fun li a la zu kurchusi ta ni. Kurchusi ta fun si a chɔ a la wilsi a chesi bɛɛŋ ni ti ni. Ni ti fun di a maga.

ref: The men taking care of the pigs ran to the town and the farms to spread the news. Then the people came out to see what had happened.
text: Ba naa funɛ nyini kurchusi ta chen, ba fun chɔ kaali dama a la ŋɔ ba naa nyɛn dooni awa ba naa ba pu kɔha ti ni. Tɔhɔn ti tima fun li a wa Yesu wati di ba la na kin naa yɛɛ.

ref: When they came to Jesus, they saw the man who had once been full of demons. He was sitting there with his clothes on and in his right mind, and they were terrified.
text: Ba fun wa na baal naa alizin tagna kinkan fun kani un ni chen laari gar a kani Yesu wati. O wa fun bu a zu galɛŋa. Hansura fun kpak ba.

ref: Everyone who had seen what had happened told about the man and the pigs.
text: Ba naa funɛ nyɛn dooni a na chenani funɛ ya alizin tagni ta awa kurchusi ta, ba fun ŋɔ ba naa wa chen.

ref: Then the people started begging Jesus to leave their part of the country.
text: Ba funɛ nu ba sɛm chen, ba wa Yesu wati a wa sumi un di o li ba pulum ni.

ref: But Jesus would not let him. Instead, he said, “Go home to your family and tell them how much the Lord has done for you and how good he has been to you.”
text: Yesu fun ŋɔ un, “Aai. Kila i dam walɛ wati a la ŋɔ ba chenani i kunyana suŋi hin a ɔm hin siki.”

ref: The man went away into the region near the ten cities known as Decapolis and began telling everyone how much Jesus had done for him. Everyone who heard what had happened was amazed. A Dying Girl and a Sick Woman (Matthew
text: O fun kaali a la ŋɔ ba naa nyɛn Dɛkapɔlis pulum wati chenani Yesu fun ya nyɛ un. O ya ba maga alaazibu. Yesu Fun Tiibi Ju Tim Nihɛɛ Nihabichɛla (Mat.

ref: But the people laughed at him. After Jesus had sent them all out of the house, he took the girl's father and mother and his three disciples and went to where she was.
text: Ba fun a mɔmsi un. O fun duŋni ba a ta, a kpa be ti nyana awa o niina awa o gantɛl tiŋsa nara batora duŋduŋ a la zu be ti dɛ chɔɔli ti. Be ti funɛ ya bun fidalɛwa.

ref: Once again Jesus got into the boat and crossed Lake Galilee. Then as he stood on the shore, a large crowd gathered around him.
text: Yesu fun zu arŋa a la ji bɛɛŋ ti jibaŋ kinkin ti. O funɛ la tel dooni, zɛmɛ kinkan fun wa a wa go un bɛɛŋ ti nɔ ni.

ref: The person in charge of the synagogue was also there. His name was Jairus, and when he saw Jesus, he went over to him. He knelt at Jesus' feet
text: Ba bar lagmi dɛ nihɛɛ digi funɛ gbaari zɛmɛ ta ni. O sɔn funɛ ya Jairus. Saha naa o fun wa na Yesu, o fun la gbiri o suwachak ni,

ref: and started begging him for help. He said, “My little daughter is about to die! Please come and touch her, so she will get well and live.”
text: a sumi un kinkan a ŋɔ, “Mu nihabichɛla la wi a daŋni o sou. Mu sumi hinu kinkan, kuwa a wa kpa i nisa a sak un ni, chen ni o zi na alaafɛu.”

ref: Jesus went with Jairus. Many people followed along and kept crowding around.
text: Yesu fun tiŋ Jairus a kaali. Nara kinkan gbanko funɛ tiŋ un aa zaksi un.

ref: In the crowd was a woman who had been bleeding for twelve years.
text: Nihaan digi funɛ nyɛn doon a kpak wichɔɔr digi bun fidalɛ. O fun ya nin ka o vɛ peti.

ref: The woman had heard about Jesus, so she came up behind him in the crowd and barely touched his clothes.
text: O fun a nu Yesu wɛ a la lisi o tuhu ni,

ref: At that moment Jesus felt power go out from him. He turned to the crowd and asked, “Who touched my clothes?”
text: Yesu fun zim di nandigi gbaŋsi unu domi o kpok ti bar digi funɛ liu. O fun burgi o gantɛl a pɛɛsi, “Anti gbaŋsi mu takta nan ni?”

ref: His disciples said to him, “Look at all these people crowding around you! How can you ask who touched you?”
text: O gantɛl tiŋsi ta fun burgi a pɛɛsi un, “I waa na zɛmɛ ta la murgi nan ni ka i pɛɛsi ya di, anti gbaŋsi hinu?”

ref: But Jesus turned to see who had touched him.
text: Yesu ha fun vɛɛn a nyini a go di o na nar gbaŋsi un chen.

ref: The woman knew what had happened to her. So she came trembling with fear and knelt down in front of Jesus. Then she told him the whole story.
text: Hansura fun kpak haan ti kinkan. O bara fun a jigiri. O fun zim di Yesu tiibi unu a fun wa gbiri o suwachak ni a sɛm o tuha chaani maga a nyɛ un.

ref: While Jesus was still speaking, some people came from Jairus' home and said, “Your daughter has died! Why bother the teacher anymore?”
text: Yesu ha funɛ vɛɛn a sɛm, nara bɔnɛ fun a li ba bar lagmi dɛ nihɛɛ dama a wa nihɛɛ ti wati a ŋɔ un, “I be nihabichɛl ti sowu. Ta bu muksi nibiksi ti di o waali.”

ref: Jesus heard what they said, and he said to Jairus, “Don't worry. Just have faith!”
text: Yesu fun nu chenani ba sɛm chen a ŋɔ nihɛɛ ti, “Ta nyɛ o muksi hin. Nyɛ i si su min.”

ref: Jesus did not let anyone go with him except Peter and the two brothers, James and John.
text: Yesu wa fun a daŋni nandigi tiŋ un amaa a nyɛ Pita, Jems awa o niinbe Jɔn ba duŋduŋ funɛ tiŋ un kaali.

ref: They went home with Jairus and saw the people crying and making a lot of noise.
text: Ba funɛ la tel ba bar lagmi dɛ nihɛɛ ti dama, ba fun a nu ba chɛɛma ka ba wi kinkan.

ref: But Jesus ordered them not to tell anyone what had happened. Then he said, “Give her something to eat.”
text: Yesu fun ŋɔ di ba ya diwe a nyɛ be ti ni o di, a fun ŋɔ ba kpok ni, “Ma ta nyɛ nandigi zim kinkin naa yɛɛ.”

ref: Jesus and his disciples crossed the lake and brought the boat to shore near the town of Gennesaret.
text: Ba fun la ji jibaŋ kinkin ti a fun la tel tɔhɔn naa ba yir Gɛnɛsarɛt a kpa arŋ ti a vɔu dooni.

ref: But Jesus said, “Prophets are honored by everyone, except the people of their hometown and their relatives and their own family.”
text: Yesu fun ŋɔ ba, “Nara maga nyɛ Koosa witiina jirimau, amaa o sɛmbiri tɔhɔn nara wa o dam walɛ awa o lul, ba wara nyɛ un jirima.”

ref: But when Herod heard about Jesus, he said, “This must be John! I had his head cut off, and now he has come back to life.”
text: Koor zen digi funɛ nyɛn doon. Ba funɛ yir un Hɛrɔd. O funɛ nu Yesu wɛ, o fun ŋɔ o titi ni, “Baal nɔ wa Jɔn naa la chaksi nara chen ya nibɛgimnɛ. Mu nyɛ ba fun teŋ o nyuha, amaa o bur si o sou niu.”

ref: After the apostles returned to Jesus, they told him everything they had done and taught.
text: Yesu nitumɛ fidalɛ, ba naa o fun tum chen, bur wa a ŋɔ un kin naa maga ba tum awa kin naa maga ba biksi nara ta.

ref: The next Sabbath he taught in the synagogue. Many of the people who heard him were amazed and asked, “How can he do all this? Where did he get such wisdom and the power to work these miracles?
text: Ba funɛ nyɛn dooni, ba hiisi wuzu fun wa tel. O fun kaali a la zu ba bar lagmi dɛ a biksi nara Koosa wɛ. Nara kinkan funɛ nyɛn doon a nu o biksi ti. O ya ba alaazibu kinkan. Ba funɛ pɛɛsi dɔŋa, “Leene baal nɔ pu sɛm nama maga? Ka leene o vɛɛn pu zima nɔ? Ka yɛrɛ o zi yaa ya a tum o tum zene nama?

ref: Isn't he the carpenter, the son of Mary? Aren't James, Joseph, Judas, and Simon his brothers? Don't his sisters still live here in our town?” The people were upset because of what he was doing.
text: O laata funɛ ya bugilsal a nyɛn bar nɔ ni chenn? O niina laata ya Mɛri chenn? Jems, Josɛf, Judas awa Simɔn laata ya o niinbisa chenn? Ya wa o toosa laata lagmi nyɛnn?” Ba wa un wa fun lɛm dɔŋ ni, domi ba funɛ zim unu.

ref: He told them, “You may take along a walking stick. But don't carry food or a traveling bag or any money.
text: O fun ŋɔ ba, “Ma wa kaa la, ma fiɛŋ ma zer buna a chi diwe, malɛ awa kapursa.

ref: If any place won't welcome you or listen to your message, leave and shake the dust from your feet as a warning to them.”
text: Amaa ma wa zu tɔ digi ka tɔhɔn ti nara vɛ la ma duuli koo a vɛ nu nyɛ ma, ma li dooni, a piisi ma naasa hɛɛra a ta, chen ni ba zi zim di a li ba nɛɛ. O wa li ma ni.”

ref: Herod had earlier married Herodias, the wife of his brother Philip. But John had told him, “It isn't right for you to take your brother's wife!” So, in order to please Herodias, Herod arrested John and put him in prison.
text: Jɔn fun te ŋɔ un, “Chenani i ya chen, o wa wer.” O funɛ sɛm chen, Hɛrɔdias fun nyɛ Hɛrɔd tum nara ba la kpak Jɔn a tɔ un dɛ.

ref: Herodias had a grudge against John and wanted to kill him. But she could not do it
text: Doone Hɛrɔdias fun a ha Jɔn a daŋ o kpu un.

ref: because Herod was afraid of John and protected him. He knew that John was a good and holy man. Even though Herod was confused by what John said, he was glad to listen to him. And he often did.
text: Hɛrɔd funɛ ɔm Jɔnu a la nyɛ un jirima, domi o funɛ zim di Jɔn ya nibin werere. Adaha o fun vɛ la nɔɔni di ba kpu un. Hali Jɔn sɛm ta fun muksi un kinkan, amaa o ha funɛ vɛɛn a daŋni o nu o sɛm tau.

ref: I swear that I will give you as much as half of my kingdom, if you want it.”
text: O fun hɛɛsi a ŋɔ, “Kin naa maga ii daŋni, ŋɔ min, n zi nyɛ hinu. Hali ii daŋni n pulum nɔ, n zi purigiu a nyɛ hin.”

ref: The girl left and asked her mother, “What do you think I should ask for?” Her mother answered, “The head of John the Baptist!”

ref: Herod was very sorry for what he had said. But he did not want to break the promise he had made in front of his guests.
text: O sɛm ta fun chɔk Hɛrɔd tuha kinkan. O wa fun bu koti a vɛ, domi o fun hɛɛsi o walɛ ta suwachak ni di o zi nyɛ un kundɔŋu.

text: a kpa sak pɛŋ ni a wa nyɛ nihabichɛl ti ni. O fun kpa la nyɛ o niin ti ni.

ref: When John's followers learned that he had been killed, they took his body and put it in a tomb. Jesus Feeds Five Thousand (Matthew

ref: Jesus replied: You are nothing but show-offs! The prophet Isaiah was right when he wrote that God had said, “All of you praise me with your words, but you never really think about me.

text: Ma wa chɔk Koosa chiksɛ ta a li ma bun kam yaali dahaa? Ma funɛ kpa bun ka yaali damatau a dau a la biksi ma bisa di ba tiŋ a.” Kin Naa Nyɛ Nibine Tuha Doŋ (Mat.

text: Ba wa un funɛ sɛm peti, ba fun wa tel o gantɛl tiŋsi ta bɔnɛ wati ka nara kinkan fun chiŋi a go ba. Ba wa Koosa chiksɛ nibiksɛ ta funɛ di amɔrɛ.

ref: They brought the boy, and as soon as the demon saw Jesus, it made the boy shake all over. He fell down and began rolling on the ground and foaming at the mouth.
text: Ba fun kpa un a wa. Alizin tagin ti funɛ wa na Yesu, o fun bukoo si kinkan a zer be ti a lɔ tɛɛnɛ ni. O fun a bilimsi. Nantɔ fukla a li o nɔ ni.

ref: “When you welcome even a child because of me, you welcome me. And when you welcome me, you welcome the one who sent me.” For or against Jesus (Luke
text: “Nar la bichɛla nɔ a du di oo tiŋ min daha, minnɛ o la du chen. Nar la min, naa tum min chene o la du.” Ba Naa La Tum Yesu Tuma Wɛ (Mat.

ref: The crowd was really surprised to see Jesus, and everyone hurried over to greet him.
text: Zɛmɛ ta funɛ wa na Yesu, o fun jɛri ba kinkan. Ba fun chɔ a la pu un a zaami.

ref: Jesus asked, “What are you arguing about?”
text: Yesu fun pɛɛsi ba, “Yɛrɛ ma wa ba di amɔrɛ chen ni?”

ref: Jesus said, “You people don't have any faith! How much longer must I be with you? Why do I have to put up with you? Bring the boy to me.”
text: Yesu fun ŋɔ un, “Ma zinan nara se wa su min. Saha yɛrɛ mu kaa nyɛn ma wati hali ma wa zim min? Saha yɛrɛ n zi ya ma kanyita? Kpa be ti kuwa mu wati.”

ref: Jesus asked the boy's father, “How long has he been like this?” The man answered, “Ever since he was a child.
text: Yesu fun pɛɛsi be ti nyana, “Saamurɛ alizin tagin ti kpak un?” O nyan ti fun ŋɔ, “O bichɛsi nɛɛ o kpak un.

ref: At once the boy's father shouted, “I do have faith! Please help me to have even more.”
text: Be ti nyan ti vaan la nɔɔni a ŋɔ, “N si suu, amaa o wa mak min. Suŋi min.”

ref: When Jesus saw that a crowd was gathering fast, he spoke sternly to the evil spirit that had kept the boy from speaking or hearing. He said, “I order you to come out of the boy! Don't ever bother him again.”
text: Ba funɛ sɛm chene, nidamata fun chɔ a wa Yesu wati. Yesu funɛ wa na ba, o fun vaan ŋɔ alizin tagin ti ni, “Hin alizin tagin naa nyɛ be nɔ ya diŋgbiti tiina awa wom, n ŋɔ hinu, kili be ti ni a ta bu nyɛn un ni.”

ref: The spirit screamed and made the boy shake all over. Then it went out of him. The boy looked dead, and almost everyone said he was.
text: Alizin tagin ti fun huusi kinkan a si un ni kinkan a li. O funɛ wa li un ni, ba nidamata fun ŋɔ di o sou.

ref: But Jesus took hold of his hand and helped him stand up.
text: Yesu fun kpak be ti nin a zer un chiŋsi.

ref: Jesus answered, “Only prayer can force out this kind of demon.” Jesus Again Speaks about His Death (Matthew
text: O fun ŋɔ ba, “Nan alizin tagin nɔ ka ma vɛ sumi Koosa nɛnɛ, ma wa kikoti a duŋni un a ta.” Yesu Fun Bu Sɛm O Sou Ti Wɛ (Mat.

ref: Jesus left with his disciples and started through Galilee. He did not want anyone to know about it,
text: Ba fun a gil dooni a la gali Galili ni a la. Ba funɛ la chene, Yesu wa fun a daŋni nandigi zim ba wɛ,
